solution.p: To get rid of all fractions in this equation, we multiple all terms with the common denominator. In this equation, the common denominator is {x}.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} {\frac{8}{x}} \amp = {3+\frac{26}{x}} \\ \frac{8}{x} \cdot x \amp = 3 \cdot x +\frac{26}{x} \cdot x \\ 8 \amp = 3 x + 26 \\ 8 \mathbf{ - 26} \amp = 3 x + 26 \mathbf{ - 26} \\ -18 \amp = 3 x \\ \frac{-18}{3} \amp = \frac{3 x}{3} \\ -6 \amp = x \\ x \amp = -6 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. By the original equation, the domain condition is x \ne 0. This doesnt conflict with the solution above, so the solution is x = -6.
